records.query: What is polyurea used for in protective coatings?
records.answer: Polyurea is often used in applications where enhanced properties such as crack bridging, toughness, corrosion or abrasion resistance are required. This includes the use of spray-applied polyurethane or polyurea elastomeric coatings on substrates like metal, concrete, wood, and plastic [5.10, p. 467-468].

Thermoplastic elastomers (TPEs) are materials that are processed in the same manner as conventional thermoplastics but give a finished article with properties and performance similar to those of a thermoset rubber [Tracton, 2007, Chapter 10, p. 10-1]. They consist of chemically cross-linked rubber phases embedded within a continuous matrix of thermoplastic polymers. This structure gives TPEs better mechanical properties compared to simple rubber/thermoplastic blends with minimal or no cross-linking [Tracton, 2007, Chapter 10, p. 10-1].

records.query: What is a zinc-rich coating used for?
records.answer: Zinc-rich coatings are used primarily for providing excellent galvanic protection in aggressive environments. They are particularly useful for protecting ship hull superstructures, marine structures, highway bridges, chemical plant equipment, and other installations exposed to high humidity and salt [Paint and coatings applications and corrosion resistance, 6. Coating Materials (Paints), p. 137-138].

The excerpts provided do not contain specific information about "corrosión por picadura" (pitted corrosion). The materials mentioned discuss general aspects of corrosion, mechanisms, and types such as chemical and electrochemical corrosion, but they do not delve into the specifics of pitted corrosion. Therefore, based on the given content, I cannot provide a detailed explanation or definition of pitted corrosion. [Libro Pinturas_y_recubrimientos_Giúdice, Unknown, p. 124-125]

Cathodic Protection (CP) is a technology used to control corrosion on the bottoms of above-ground storage tanks that are in contact with sand pads or soil. It works through an electrochemical process where direct electrical current is passed to the metal surface from electrodes installed in the electrolyte adjacent to, or near, the tank [Cathodic_Protection, Unknown, p. 1].

This method involves creating a galvanic cell by connecting a more reactive (anode) material to the structure that needs protection (cathode). The current flows from the anode through the electrolyte to the cathode, which reduces the corrosion rate of the protected metal [Libro Corrosion of Linings and Coatings - Schweitzer, Chapter 2. Cathodic Protection, p. 60-61].

The effectiveness of CP can be measured using tank-to-soil potential measurements taken close to the tank bottom, though this requires precise placement of a reference electrode near the protected area [Cathodic_Protection, Unknown, p. 1].

La corrosión galvánica es un tipo de corrosión acelerada que ocurre cuando se produce un contacto eléctrico entre dos metales disímiles en un electrólito. En este proceso, un metal (llamado ánodo galvánico) proporciona protección sacrificial a otro metal más noble al actuar como fuente de electrones [GALVANIC ANODE, GLOSSARY, p. 12]. Este tipo de corrosión es el resultado de la diferencia en las potencialidades de corrupción entre los metales [GALVANIC SERIES, GLOSSARY, p. 12].

Además, se describe que la corrosión galvánica puede presentarse en diferentes formas, como ataque uniforme o ataque de surcos [CORROSION, DEPOSITION TECHNOLOGIES FOR FILM AND COATINGS, p. 766-767].

Stress corrosion cracking (SCC) develops when a tough polymer is stressed for an extended period under loads that are small relative to the polymer’s yield point [Corrosion of Linings and Coatings, Schweitzer, p. 309]. The higher the molecular weight of the polymer, other things being equal, the less likelihood of environmental stress cracking [ibid, p. 310-311]. Additionally, factors such as the presence of contaminants in a fluid may act as an accelerator for SCC; for example, polypropylene can safely handle sulfuric or hydrochloric acids, but iron or copper contamination in concentrated solutions of these acids can result in stress cracking [ibid, p. 310].

The mechanism involves tensile stresses and corrosive media working together to induce cracks. Environmental factors like temperature and the presence of specific alloying elements also play a role; for instance, certain mechanical treatments can induce high residual stresses that may lead to SCC initiation in materials [9711mpab, Streicher, p. 2].

In zirconium alloys, stress corrosion cracking (SCC) has been observed in concentrated methanol solutions containing heavy metal chlorides, gaseous iodine, or fused salts with iodine, even though these materials are generally resistant to SCC in other environments [Handbook of Corrosion Engineering, p. 357].

Los inhibidores de corrosión son sustancias químicas que, cuando se añaden en concentraciones pequeñas a un entorno, eficazmente reducen la tasa de corrosión. [Handbook of Corrosion Engineering, Corrosion Inhibitors, p. 839-840]
